title: XAS / EXAFS — X-ray Absorption & Extended X-ray Absorption Fine Structure
sub_domain: Inner-shell X-ray absorption + multiple scattering oscillations
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence boilerplate. Physics grounded in Sayers-Stern-Lytle 1971 EXAFS seminal paper; Rehr-Albers 2000 RMP review; Newville 2014 fundamentals; Koningsberger-Prins 1988 XAS principles book.

E.description: Above an absorption edge (K, L1-3), an X-ray photon ejects a core-level photoelectron whose outgoing spherical wave back-scatters off neighboring atoms, interfering with the outgoing wave. This creates oscillations chi(k) in the absorption coefficient vs. wavenumber k = sqrt(2m(E-E_0))/hbar. XANES (<50 eV above edge) probes oxidation/coordination; EXAFS (>50 eV) probes radial distribution functions.
E.forward_model: mu(E) = mu_0(E) * [1 + chi(k)];  chi(k) = sum_j (N_j S_0^2 / (k R_j^2)) * F_j(k) * exp(-2 sigma_j^2 k^2) * sin(2 k R_j + phi_j(k))
E.world_state_x: Radial coordination parameters {R_j, N_j, sigma_j^2, E_0 shift}
E.observation_y: Absorption coefficient mu(E) measured via transmission or fluorescence yield
physical_parameters_theta: Energy step delta_E and range above edge
Signal-to-noise (depends on beam flux and detector)

W.uniqueness: structural parameters unique when Nyquist-sampled in k-space and shells non-degenerate
W.regime: Fourier-transform EXAFS inversion is linear; parameter fitting (R_j, N_j, sigma_j^2) is nonlinear; correlation among N, S_0, sigma is a major ill-conditioning source.

C.solver_class: FEFF path calculation + Artemis / IFEFFIT nonlinear LS fit; wavelet transform; learned (XANES-Net, EXAFS-CNN)
C.error_bound: R uncertainty ~ 1/(2*k_max) = 0.05 A at k_max=10 A^-1; N uncertainty ~20% due to S_0^2 correlation
C.complexity: FEFF O(N_path * N_energy); Artemis fit O(iter * N_param * N_E)
